quote: Morphological identity: causative and applicative morphology
issue: Section title promises discussion of both causative and applicative morphology, but the content only covers causative suffixes (-re, -e, -ka). No applicative morphology is mentioned anywhere in the section.
suggestion: Either add discussion of applicative morphology or revise the title to 'causative morphology'.

quote: a=kor cise poro=an 'my house is big' (constructed)
issue: The Ainu form uses the prefix a=, which is fourth person (indefinite/one's) or first person plural, not first person singular. Earlier in the chapter (line 124) first person singular is consistently ku=. The translation 'my' therefore contradicts the person-marking system described.
suggestion: Change the Ainu form to ku=kor cise poro=an or adjust the translation to 'one's house is big' / 'our house is big'.

quote: en=pirka akkari pirka=an 'you are more beautiful than I am'
issue: The verb pirka is intransitive and should take an S-prefix (e.g., ku=), but here it carries the object prefix en=. This contradicts the chapter's core claim that property verbs take intransitive-subject prefixes. The description says akkari takes an object-marked antecedent, but the antecedent en=pirka appears to be a clause with an object prefix on an intransitive verb, which is not a standard pattern.
suggestion: Revise the example to use a standard comparative construction, e.g., ku=pirka akkari e=pirka=an (if that is grammatical) or provide a glossed example from a source.
